questions.question: What are the three components of the Smallville generative-agent architecture?
options: Memory stream (append-only natural-language log), reflection (higher-order syntheses fed back into the stream), and plan (day-level decomposed to hour and action level, revisable)
questions.explanation: Park et al. 2023 is the reference architecture for open-world agent simulation. Ablations show all three are required: no observation means stale beliefs, no reflection means shallow interactions, no plan means reactive noise.

questions.question: How does memory retrieval score entries in the Smallville architecture?
options: A weighted combination of recency (exponential decay), importance (self-rated 1-10), and relevance (cosine similarity to the current query) — top-k enter the prompt
questions.explanation: score = w_recency·e^(−decay·age) + w_importance·importance + w_relevance·cos_sim. This three-factor retrieval is what lets a 25-agent simulation stay coherent without stuffing whole histories into context.

questions.question: Why is the Valentine's Day party result a landmark?
options: It was the first LLM to mention a holiday

One seeded agent's party intent spread through 24 unseeded agents via invitations becoming observations, reflections becoming beliefs, and plans incorporating attendance — system-level behavior from purely local interactions, no orchestrator
It proved agents can simulate cooking
questions.explanation: This is emergence in the technical sense: invitations propagated agent-to-agent, dates got coordinated, and agents converged at Hobbs Cafe at 5pm — with no central script beyond Isabella's initial goal.

questions.question: Which failure modes did Park et al. document that production simulations still inherit?
options: Spatial-norm errors (entering closed stores, sharing one-person bathrooms), memory overflow as retrieval costs grow, and reflection hallucination inventing relationships absent from the stream
questions.explanation: Mitigations are practical: periodic memory compaction with decay on low-importance entries, and source-memory ids inside reflection prompts so invented syntheses can be verified at retrieval time.

questions.question: What distinguishes generative-agent simulation from scripted multi-agent teams?
options: Scripted teams (planner/coder/reviewer) suit well-defined tasks; generative agents with memory, priorities, and an open world produce emergent unscripted behavior — needed for research, society simulation, and game AI
There is no difference in practice
questions.explanation: Since Park 2023, building an open-world agent simulation means either using the three Smallville components or explicitly justifying why not — it became the default pattern for the second kind of multi-agent system.
